name: tourism
title: Tourism & Hospitality
description: Adapts the harness to produce tour packages and itinerary proposals — multi-day trips, group tours, bespoke travel — where the failure mode is an infeasible schedule, a price that does not add up, or an unsupported marketing claim, not a failing build.

stages.label: Frame
stages.purpose: Define the client/segment, the travel dates, the budget, and the constraints (group size, accessibility, restricted activities, visa/seasonal limits).
stages.output: Scope note: client, segment, dates, budget, decision needed

stages.label: Plan
stages.purpose: Design the itinerary and logistics, select suppliers, and fix every timing and price before any proposal text is written. The plan the draft must follow.

stages.label: Draft
stages.purpose: Write the proposal: itinerary, inclusions, pricing, narrative — each price sourced to a supplier quote, each claim substantiated.

stages.label: Review breadth
stages.purpose: Scan every section for completeness and internal consistency; flag missing sections, unpriced line items, unsupported marketing claims.

stages.label: Review depth
stages.purpose: Run the gate verdicts: logistics feasibility, pricing reconciliation, claim substantiation, safety & compliance, client fit.

stages.label: Feedback
stages.purpose: Evaluate reviewer challenges, reconcile the quote, finalize safety and cancellation terms.

gates.name: Logistics Feasibility
gates.question: Are the timings, transfers, and opening hours realistic — does the itinerary physically work within each day and across the trip?

gates.name: Pricing Reconciliation
gates.question: Do the priced line items sum to the quoted total — none missing, double-counted, or fudged?

gates.name: Claim Substantiation
gates.question: Is every marketing claim sourced or factual (not puffery) — superlatives and figures carry a citation or supplier source?

gates.name: Safety & Compliance
gates.question: Are insurance, cancellation policy, and relevant travel advisories present and specific (not boilerplate)?

gates.name: Client Fit
gates.question: Does the package match the client's segment, budget, and accessibility needs, and are reusable itinerary components factored out rather than re-derived?
